phases.title: Foundation
phases.subtitle: HTML & CSS — The Web's Skeleton
phases.description: Everything on the web is HTML and CSS. Before frameworks, before JavaScript — understand how browsers render content, how the box model works, and how to structure information semantically. This is your bedrock.

sections.title: HTML Fundamentals
sections.subtitle: Structure & Semantics
topics: DOCTYPE, head, body, meta tags
Semantic elements: header, nav, main, section, article, footer
Headings, paragraphs, lists, links, images
Forms: input, textarea, select, button, label
Tables, HTML attributes, data attributes, ARIA roles
How browsers parse and render HTML (critical rendering path)

sections.title: CSS Core
sections.subtitle: Selectors & Box Model
topics: Selectors: element, class, ID, combinators, pseudo-classes
Box model: margin, padding, border, content-box vs border-box
Display types: block, inline, inline-block, none
Positioning: static, relative, absolute, fixed, sticky
CSS specificity and the cascade
Units: px, %, em, rem, vw, vh — CSS variables

sections.title: CSS Layouts
sections.subtitle: Flexbox & CSS Grid
topics: Flexbox: flex-direction, justify-content, align-items, flex-wrap
Flexbox: flex-grow, flex-shrink, flex-basis, order
CSS Grid: grid-template-columns/rows, fr unit, grid-gap
Grid areas, named lines, auto-fill vs auto-fit
When to use Flexbox vs Grid — the key mental model

sections.title: Responsive Design
sections.subtitle: Mobile-First Development
topics: Viewport meta tag and why it matters
Media queries: min-width mobile-first approach
Responsive images: srcset, sizes, aspect-ratio
Fluid typography with clamp()
CSS transitions and animations (@keyframes)
CSS transforms: translate, scale, rotate

projects: Build a complete portfolio landing page from scratch
Clone any popular website homepage pixel-perfect
Build a multi-page responsive static business website
outcomes: Build any layout from a Figma design without help
Make any layout work on mobile without Googling
Explain CSS specificity and the cascade confidently

phases.title: Core Language
phases.subtitle: JavaScript — The Brain of the Web
phases.description: JavaScript is the only programming language of the browser. Master it deeply before touching any framework. React, Vue, Node — they are all just JavaScript. Weak JS fundamentals will haunt you in every technical interview.

sections.title: JS Foundations
sections.subtitle: Variables, Types & Control Flow
topics: var vs let vs const — hoisting and temporal dead zone
Data types: string, number, boolean, null, undefined, symbol
Type coercion: == vs === and why this matters
Control flow: if/else, switch, ternary operator
Loops: for, while, for...of, for...in
Functions: declarations, expressions, arrow functions, scope

sections.title: JS Deep Dives
sections.subtitle: Arrays, Objects & Closures
topics: Arrays: map, filter, reduce, find, forEach, some, every
Array and object destructuring, spread/rest operators
Object creation, methods, shorthand syntax
Closures — the most important JS concept for interviews
The 'this' keyword in different contexts
Prototype chain and how inheritance works

sections.title: Async JavaScript
sections.subtitle: Promises, Async/Await & APIs
topics: The Event Loop — how JS handles concurrency
Callbacks and why Promises were invented
Promises: .then(), .catch(), .finally(), Promise.all()
async/await syntax — the modern standard
fetch() API for HTTP requests, JSON parse and stringify
Error handling with try/catch in async code

sections.title: DOM Manipulation
sections.subtitle: Interacting with the Browser
topics: querySelector, querySelectorAll, getElementById
createElement, appendChild, innerHTML vs textContent
Event listeners: click, submit, keydown, input, change
Event bubbling and event delegation pattern
Forms: prevent default, read values, validation
localStorage and sessionStorage

sections.title: ES6+ Modern JS
sections.subtitle: Current JavaScript Standards
topics: Template literals, tagged templates
Modules: import/export (ESModules)
Classes: constructor, methods, inheritance, static
Optional chaining ?. and nullish coalescing ??
WeakMap, WeakSet, Map, Set data structures

sections.title: Browser DevTools
sections.subtitle: Debugging Mastery
topics: Console: log, error, warn, table, time, group
Setting breakpoints and stepping through code
Network tab: inspect API calls, headers, responses
Elements tab: live-edit HTML/CSS for debugging
Performance tab: identify rendering bottlenecks

projects: Todo App with localStorage (CRUD + filter + categories)
Weather App using fetch + real API (async/await)
JavaScript Quiz Game (DOM + state management)
Expense Tracker (arrays + localStorage + charts)
outcomes: Explain the event loop on a whiteboard
Write a debounce/throttle function from scratch
Fetch data from any API and handle all error states
Build a complete DOM app without any framework

phases.title: Frontend Framework
phases.subtitle: React — The Industry Standard
phases.description: React powers 40% or more of all web apps. It is the most-hired frontend skill in 2025. Learn it properly — components, hooks, state management, and data fetching. This is where you become employable as a frontend developer.

sections.title: React Core
sections.subtitle: Components & JSX
topics: What React is and why it exists (Virtual DOM concept)
JSX: JavaScript + HTML syntax, expressions in JSX
Functional components — forget class components in 2025
Props: passing data down, default props, prop validation
Children prop and component composition patterns
Conditional rendering: &&, ternary, early return

sections.title: React Hooks
sections.subtitle: State & Side Effects
topics: useState: local state, updater functions, state batching
useEffect: deps array, cleanup functions, rules of hooks
useRef: DOM refs and persisting values across renders
useMemo and useCallback: optimization (do not overuse)
useContext: context API to solve prop drilling
useReducer: for complex state logic, custom hooks

sections.title: React Router v6
sections.subtitle: Client-Side Routing
topics: BrowserRouter, Routes, Route component setup
Link and NavLink for navigation
useNavigate, useParams, useLocation hooks
Nested routes and shared layout components
Protected routes with authentication guards
Dynamic routes and programmatic navigation

sections.title: Data Fetching
sections.subtitle: APIs in React
topics: Fetching on mount with useEffect (the manual way)
Loading states, error states, empty states pattern
TanStack Query (React Query) — industry standard tool
useQuery, useMutation, query invalidation pattern
Axios vs fetch — when to use each
Optimistic updates pattern for better UX

sections.title: Styling in React
sections.subtitle: CSS Approaches
topics: CSS Modules: scoped styles, how to import and use
Tailwind CSS: utility-first, responsive modifiers (prioritize this)
shadcn/ui component library — setup and usage
Lucide icons, Radix UI accessible primitives
Styled-components/Emotion (CSS-in-JS) — know it exists

sections.title: State Management
sections.subtitle: Beyond useState
topics: When do you actually need global state (honest answer)
Context API for simple global state
Zustand: lightweight modern choice (recommended)
Redux Toolkit: for larger apps and legacy codebases
Server state vs client state — the key mental model

projects: E-commerce product listing with cart (state management)
Movie search app using TMDB API (TanStack Query)
Personal finance dashboard (charts + Tailwind)
Full CRUD app with routing and protected pages

timeline.milestone: Deploy a portfolio site to Netlify or Vercel
Build a working weather app and a CRUD todo app
START applying for Junior Frontend Developer jobs

phases.title: Backend & Databases
phases.subtitle: Node.js, Express & Databases
phases.description: Becoming full-stack means understanding servers, APIs, and databases. Node.js lets you use the JavaScript you already know on the server. Add SQL and you can build complete applications entirely end-to-end.

sections.title: Node.js
sections.subtitle: JavaScript on the Server
topics: Node.js architecture: event loop, non-blocking I/O
npm, npx, package.json, node_modules, .gitignore
CommonJS vs ES Modules in Node environment
Built-in modules: fs, path, http, os, events
Environment variables with dotenv (.env files)
Nodemon for development, PM2 for production

sections.title: Express.js
sections.subtitle: Building REST APIs
topics: REST principles: resources, HTTP methods, status codes
Express setup: routing, middleware, req/res objects
Route parameters, query strings, request body parsing
Error handling middleware (the 4-parameter signature)
CORS: what it is, why it happens, how to configure
JWT authentication: sign, verify, refresh token rotation
Input validation with Zod or Joi libraries

sections.title: SQL & PostgreSQL
sections.subtitle: Relational Databases
topics: Relational model: tables, rows, columns, primary keys
SQL: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, WHERE clauses
JOINs: INNER, LEFT, RIGHT — when to use each type
Indexes: what they are, when to create them
Transactions: ACID properties, BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK
Database design: normalization, foreign keys, constraints
PostgreSQL setup locally and on cloud (Supabase or Neon)

sections.title: ORM / Query Builder
sections.subtitle: Prisma or Drizzle
topics: Prisma: schema definition, migrations, Prisma Client
CRUD with Prisma: create, findMany, update, delete
Relations in Prisma: one-to-many, many-to-many
Drizzle ORM: SQL-like syntax, type safety (modern choice)
When to write raw SQL vs use an ORM — the honest answer

sections.title: Authentication
sections.subtitle: Security Fundamentals
topics: Password hashing with bcrypt — never store plaintext
JWT access tokens + refresh token rotation pattern
Cookie-based sessions vs localStorage JWT tradeoffs
OAuth 2.0 flow: Login with Google implementation
Role-based access control (RBAC) patterns
HTTPS, CORS, CSRF, XSS — interview-level knowledge

sections.title: NoSQL Option
sections.subtitle: MongoDB (When to Use It)
topics: Document model vs relational model differences
When MongoDB makes sense (flexible schema, rapid prototyping)
MongoDB Atlas free cloud database setup
Mongoose ODM: schemas, models, validation, queries
Aggregation pipeline for complex query operations
Honest take: PostgreSQL is more versatile for most apps

projects: Full REST API with JWT auth and PostgreSQL database
Blog platform with CMS features (complete CRUD)
Twitter/X clone backend with complex table relations

phases.title: Modern Full-Stack
phases.subtitle: Next.js, TypeScript & Deployment
phases.description: Next.js is the dominant full-stack React framework. TypeScript is now a baseline expectation in 2025. Together with modern deployment tools, you become a production-grade engineer capable of shipping real products.

sections.title: TypeScript
sections.subtitle: Type-Safe JavaScript
topics: Basic types: string, number, boolean, arrays, tuples
Interfaces vs Types — when to use each one
Generics: writing reusable typed code
Union types, intersection types, discriminated unions
Type narrowing: typeof, instanceof, in operator
Utility types: Partial, Required, Pick, Omit, Record
TypeScript with React: typing props, hooks, and events

sections.title: Next.js App Router
sections.subtitle: The Full-Stack Framework
topics: App Router architecture vs Pages Router (use App Router)
Server Components vs Client Components — the key distinction
File-based routing: pages, layouts, loading, error files
Server Actions: mutations without separate API routes
API Routes and Route Handlers for custom endpoints
Metadata API for SEO, dynamic metadata generation
Image optimization with the next/image component

sections.title: Rendering Strategies
sections.subtitle: SSR, SSG, ISR & Streaming
topics: SSR (Server-Side Rendering): when data must be fresh
SSG (Static Site Generation): for content sites and blogs
ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration): cached but revalidated
Streaming with Suspense to improve Time to First Byte
fetch() with revalidate and cache options in Next.js
Understanding Next.js complex caching layers

sections.title: Tooling & Testing
sections.subtitle: Professional Dev Workflow
topics: Git: branching strategies, merging, rebasing, stash
GitHub: PRs, code review, Issues, Actions for CI/CD
ESLint + Prettier: automated code quality enforcement
Husky + lint-staged: pre-commit hooks setup
Vitest and React Testing Library for unit/integration tests
Playwright or Cypress for end-to-end browser testing

sections.title: Deployment
sections.subtitle: Shipping to Production
topics: Vercel: instant Next.js deployment, edge functions
Netlify, Railway, Render as solid alternatives
Environment variables management in production
Domain configuration and DNS basics
Docker: containerize any application (essential for backend)
Docker Compose: multi-service local development environments
Basic Linux CLI for remote server management via SSH

sections.title: Auth Solutions
sections.subtitle: Production Auth in Next.js
topics: NextAuth.js / Auth.js: the community standard solution
Clerk: managed auth with UI components (fastest to ship)
Supabase Auth: if you're already using Supabase
Sessions, cookies, and JWT handling in Next.js
Middleware for protecting routes at the edge
Email/password + multiple OAuth providers

projects: SaaS app with auth, Stripe payments, and dashboard
Full e-commerce (cart, checkout, orders) complete stack
Real-time chat application (WebSockets + Prisma)

phases.title: Advanced / Senior Level
phases.subtitle: System Design, Performance & Scale
phases.description: Senior engineers don't just write features — they design systems. This phase covers what separates mid-level from senior: performance optimization, scalable architecture, cloud infrastructure, and engineering leadership.

sections.title: Performance
sections.subtitle: Web Performance Engineering
topics: Core Web Vitals: LCP, FID/INP, CLS — measure and optimize
Code splitting, lazy loading, dynamic imports
Bundle analysis with webpack-bundle-analyzer
Image optimization: WebP, AVIF, responsive images
Caching strategies: HTTP cache, CDN, service workers
Database query optimization: EXPLAIN ANALYZE, N+1 problem
Redis for caching, sessions, and pub/sub

sections.title: Architecture
sections.subtitle: System Design Patterns
topics: REST vs GraphQL vs tRPC — choosing the right API style
Microservices vs Monolith — honest engineering tradeoffs
Event-driven architecture with message queues (BullMQ)
WebSockets and real-time application patterns
Database scaling: read replicas, sharding concepts
CQRS and Event Sourcing patterns
Rate limiting, circuit breakers, and bulkheads

sections.title: Cloud & DevOps
sections.subtitle: AWS / Cloud Infrastructure
topics: AWS core services: EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda, CloudFront
Infrastructure as Code with Terraform or AWS CDK
Kubernetes basics: pods, deployments, services (understand it)
GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline construction
Monitoring: Sentry for errors, Datadog or Grafana+Prometheus
Log management: CloudWatch, Logtail
AWS Solutions Architect Associate cert (optional but valued)

sections.title: AI Integration
sections.subtitle: LLMs in Web Apps (2025 Essential)
topics: OpenAI API, Anthropic API: text, vision, structured outputs
Vercel AI SDK for streaming AI responses in Next.js
RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) with vector databases
Embedding models and semantic search implementation
AI agents with tool use and function calling
Prompt engineering as a core product skill
Cost optimization for LLM API calls at scale

sections.title: Quality Engineering
sections.subtitle: Testing at Scale
topics: Testing pyramid: unit, integration, end-to-end balance
Vitest: fast unit testing for modern JavaScript
React Testing Library: test behavior not implementation details
MSW (Mock Service Worker): mock APIs in test environments
Playwright: end-to-end browser automation
Test-Driven Development: when it helps and when it doesn't

sections.title: Engineering Leadership
sections.subtitle: Soft Skills That Ship
topics: Writing technical design documents (RFCs)
Code review culture: giving and receiving feedback well
Estimating work and communicating uncertainty to stakeholders
Mentoring junior developers effectively
Incident response, owning outages, post-mortems
Open source contributions as a career accelerant

projects: Build and launch a SaaS product with real users
Contribute meaningfully to a major open-source project
Pass a system design interview: design Twitter from scratch
outcomes: CTO at a startup — $150K+ with equity

Build a working weather app and a CRUD todo app
START applying for Junior Frontend Developer jobs

Apply for Full-Stack Engineer roles in the $100K+ range
Targeting Senior Engineer roles at $140K and above

phases.title: Foundation
phases.subtitle: Python & Mathematics for ML
phases.description: Python is the language of AI. Mathematics is its grammar. Before touching neural networks or datasets, build rock-solid foundations in Python programming and the three mathematical pillars of ML: linear algebra, calculus, and probability.

sections.title: Python for ML
sections.subtitle: Programming Foundations
topics: Variables, data types, control flow, functions, scope
Lists, tuples, dicts, sets — Python's core data structures
List comprehensions, generators, iterators
OOP: classes, inheritance, dunder/magic methods
File I/O: reading CSV, JSON, text files
Virtual environments: venv, conda, pip package management
Jupyter Notebooks: the ML engineer's primary workspace

sections.title: Linear Algebra
sections.subtitle: Vectors, Matrices & Tensors
topics: Scalars, vectors, matrices, tensors — definitions and intuition
Matrix multiplication: the core operation of deep learning
Dot products, cross products, vector norms
Matrix inversion, transpose, determinants
Eigenvalues and eigenvectors (critical for PCA)
Singular Value Decomposition (SVD)
NumPy: implementing all concepts in code immediately

sections.title: Calculus
sections.subtitle: Derivatives & Optimization
topics: Derivatives: rate of change, slope intuition
Chain rule — the backbone of backpropagation
Partial derivatives and gradients
Gradient descent: intuition and implementation
Learning rates, local vs global minima
Multivariable calculus: Jacobians, Hessians (conceptual)
Implement gradient descent from scratch in pure NumPy

sections.title: Probability & Statistics
sections.subtitle: The Language of Uncertainty
topics: Probability: axioms, conditional probability, Bayes' theorem
Distributions: Normal, Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson
Expectation, variance, covariance, correlation
Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE)
Hypothesis testing, p-values, confidence intervals
Information theory: entropy, KL divergence
Central Limit Theorem and its implications for ML

projects: Implement matrix multiplication from scratch without NumPy
Build a gradient descent optimizer in pure Python
Statistical analysis report on a real-world dataset (Jupyter)
outcomes: Derive backpropagation on a whiteboard without notes
Explain eigenvalues and their purpose intuitively
Implement Bayes' theorem correctly in a practical example

phases.title: Data Science Core
phases.subtitle: Data Manipulation & Visualization
phases.description: Before modeling anything, you must master the art of understanding data. EDA (Exploratory Data Analysis) is how every successful ML project begins. NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, and Seaborn are daily instruments for every AI/ML engineer.

sections.title: NumPy
sections.subtitle: Numerical Computing
topics: ndarray: creation, shape, dtype, dimensions
Indexing, slicing, boolean masking
Broadcasting rules — the most powerful NumPy concept
Universal functions (ufuncs): vectorized operations
Linear algebra with np.linalg
Random number generation and seeding for reproducibility
Memory layout: C vs Fortran order, views vs copies

sections.title: Pandas
sections.subtitle: Data Wrangling
topics: Series and DataFrame: creation, inspection, dtypes
Reading data: CSV, JSON, Excel, SQL, Parquet
Indexing: .loc, .iloc, boolean indexing
Handling missing data: isnull, fillna, dropna strategies
GroupBy: the split-apply-combine pattern
Merging and joining DataFrames (inner, outer, left, right)
Time series: date parsing, resampling, rolling windows

sections.title: Visualization
sections.subtitle: Matplotlib & Seaborn
topics: Matplotlib: figure, axes, subplots architecture
Line, scatter, bar, histogram, pie charts from scratch
Seaborn: statistical visualizations with less code
Heatmaps for correlation matrices
Distribution plots: violin, box, KDE
Plotly for interactive visualizations
Principles of data visualization (Tufte's rules)

sections.title: Exploratory Data Analysis
sections.subtitle: Understanding Your Data
topics: Descriptive statistics: mean, median, mode, std, percentiles
Distribution analysis: skewness, kurtosis, outlier detection
Correlation analysis: Pearson, Spearman, point-biserial
Feature relationships: scatter matrices, pair plots
Data quality checks: duplicates, inconsistencies, class balance
EDA report workflow: a repeatable investigation process
ydata-profiling: automated EDA report generation

projects: Full EDA on Titanic or House Prices dataset (Kaggle)
Time series analysis of stock or weather data using Pandas
Automated data cleaning pipeline on a messy real dataset
outcomes: Clean any messy real-world dataset in under 2 hours
Identify data problems before they poison a model
Publish a full EDA notebook on Kaggle publicly

phases.title: Classical Machine Learning
phases.subtitle: Scikit-learn & The ML Fundamentals
phases.description: Classical ML algorithms are the interview gauntlet and the production workhorse. Many real-world problems are best solved with a well-tuned gradient boosted tree, not a transformer. Master every algorithm, its assumptions, its failure modes, and when to use it.

sections.title: Supervised Learning
sections.subtitle: Regression & Classification
topics: Linear Regression: OLS, assumptions, interpretation of coefficients
Logistic Regression: decision boundary, log-odds, sigmoid
Decision Trees: splitting criteria, depth control, pruning
Random Forest: bagging, feature importance, OOB error
Gradient Boosting: XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost (top interview topics)
Support Vector Machines: kernel trick, margins, C parameter
K-Nearest Neighbors: curse of dimensionality, distance metrics

sections.title: Model Evaluation
sections.subtitle: Metrics & Validation
topics: Train/validation/test split — why 3 sets, not 2
Cross-validation: k-fold, stratified k-fold, time-series split
Classification metrics: accuracy, precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC
Regression metrics: MSE, RMSE, MAE, R-squared, adjusted R-squared
Confusion matrix: interpreting TP, FP, FN, TN correctly
Bias-variance tradeoff: the fundamental ML dilemma
Learning curves: diagnosing underfitting vs overfitting visually

sections.title: Feature Engineering
sections.subtitle: The Most Impactful Skill
topics: Encoding: one-hot, ordinal, target, binary encoding
Scaling: StandardScaler, MinMaxScaler, RobustScaler
Handling missing values: imputation strategies
Feature creation: polynomial, interaction, domain-specific
Feature selection: correlation, mutual information, RFECV

sections.title: Causal Inference

Randomized controlled trials (RCTs): the gold standard
Difference-in-differences (DiD): before/after treatment analysis

Regression discontinuity design (RDD)
Propensity score matching and weighting

phases.title: Foundation
phases.subtitle: Linux, Networking & Scripting Basics
phases.description: DevOps is built on three non-negotiable foundations: Linux, networking, and scripting. Every cloud server runs Linux. Every deployment crosses a network. Every automation requires scripting. These are not optional prerequisites — they are the daily tools you will use for your entire career.

sections.title: Linux Fundamentals
sections.subtitle: The Operating System of the Cloud
topics: Linux distributions: Ubuntu, CentOS, RHEL, Amazon Linux — differences and use cases
Filesystem hierarchy: /, /etc, /var, /home, /opt, /tmp — what lives where
File and directory operations: ls, cd, cp, mv, rm, mkdir, find, locate
File permissions: chmod, chown, chgrp — read/write/execute, octal notation
Users and groups: useradd, usermod, passwd, sudo, /etc/passwd, /etc/sudoers
Process management: ps, top, htop, kill, pkill, nice, jobs, bg, fg
Package management: apt (Debian/Ubuntu), yum/dnf (RHEL/CentOS), package repos
Systemd and services: systemctl start/stop/enable/status, journalctl for logs
Disk management: df, du, mount, umount, fdisk, lsblk, /etc/fstab
Text editors: vim basics (insert, save, quit), nano for quick edits

sections.title: Linux CLI Mastery
sections.subtitle: Command Line Power Tools
topics: Text processing: grep, sed, awk, cut, sort, uniq, wc, head, tail
Pipes and redirection: |, >, >>, <, 2>, 2>&1, /dev/null
File viewing: cat, less, more, diff, wc
Archiving and compression: tar, gzip, gunzip, zip, unzip
Environment variables: export, .bashrc, .bash_profile, .profile, PATH
Cron jobs: crontab -e, cron syntax (minute/hour/day/month/weekday), cron.d
SSH: ssh, ssh-keygen, ssh-copy-id, ~/.ssh/config, key-based authentication
SCP and rsync: secure file transfer between servers
Screen and tmux: persistent terminal sessions, multiplexing
curl and wget: downloading files, testing HTTP endpoints from CLI

sections.title: Networking Fundamentals
sections.subtitle: How the Internet Actually Works
topics: OSI model: all 7 layers and what happens at each — not just memorization
TCP/IP: how packets travel, three-way handshake, connection states
IP addressing: IPv4 classes, CIDR notation, subnetting (calculate /24, /16, /28)
DNS: A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, NS records — how resolution works end to end
HTTP/HTTPS: methods (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE), status codes, headers, TLS handshake
Common ports: 22 (SSH), 80 (HTTP), 443 (HTTPS), 3306 (MySQL), 5432 (Postgres), 6379 (Redis)
Firewalls: iptables basics, ufw (Uncomplicated Firewall), inbound/outbound rules
Load balancing concepts: round-robin, least connections, health checks
NAT and proxies: how traffic is routed through private networks
Network debugging: ping, traceroute, netstat, ss, nslookup, dig, tcpdump basics

sections.title: Shell Scripting
sections.subtitle: Automating Everything with Bash
topics: Bash script structure: shebang (#!/bin/bash), permissions, execution
Variables: declaration, quoting, command substitution ($(), ``)
Conditionals: if/elif/else, test operators (-z, -n, -f, -d, -eq, -ne, -lt, -gt)
Loops: for, while, until, break, continue, loop over files and arrays
Functions: definition, arguments ($1, $2, $@), return values
String manipulation: substring, length, replace, split
Arrays: indexed arrays, associative arrays, iterating
Input/Output: read user input, here-documents (<<EOF), printf
Exit codes: $?, trap for error handling, set -e, set -u, set -o pipefail
Script best practices: logging, error handling, idempotency, dry-run flags

projects: Write a bash script that automates server setup: installs packages, creates users, sets up SSH keys
Build a log rotation and archiving script with cron scheduling
Create a system health monitoring script that alerts when CPU/memory/disk exceeds thresholds
Set up a Linux VM from scratch (VirtualBox or Multipass) and configure it entirely via CLI
outcomes: Navigate and manage any Linux server without a GUI

Debug network connectivity issues using CLI tools
Configure users, permissions, services, and cron jobs confidently

phases.title: Version Control & Collaboration
phases.subtitle: Git, GitHub & Team Workflows
phases.description: Git is the foundation of every DevOps workflow. Infrastructure as Code, CI/CD pipelines, and application deployments all start with a git push. Master it completely — not just the basics, but the branching strategies and team workflows that real engineering teams use.

sections.title: Git Core
sections.subtitle: Version Control Fundamentals
topics: Git internals: working tree, staging area (index), local repository, remote repository
Core commands: init, clone, add, commit, status, log, diff, show
Branching: branch, checkout, switch — HEAD and detached HEAD state
Merging: merge (fast-forward vs 3-way), merge conflicts — resolving them correctly
Rebasing: rebase vs merge, interactive rebase (squash, fixup, reword, edit)
Remote operations: remote, fetch, pull, push, upstream tracking
Stashing: stash, stash pop, stash list, stash apply — saving work in progress
Tagging: lightweight vs annotated tags, semantic versioning (v1.2.3)
Undoing changes: reset (soft/mixed/hard), revert, restore, clean
Git log mastery: --oneline, --graph, --author, --since, --grep, --all

sections.title: Branching Strategies
sections.subtitle: Team Workflows at Scale
topics: GitFlow: main, develop, feature/*, release/*, hotfix/* — when it fits
Trunk-based development: short-lived feature branches, feature flags — the modern standard
GitHub Flow: simple branch → PR → merge pattern for CI/CD teams
Branch naming conventions: feat/, fix/, chore/, docs/, refactor/
Commit message conventions: Conventional Commits (feat:, fix:, chore:, BREAKING CHANGE:)
Monorepo vs polyrepo: tradeoffs for DevOps teams
Git hooks: pre-commit, pre-push, commit-msg — automating quality checks locally

sections.title: GitHub / GitLab for DevOps
sections.subtitle: Collaboration & Automation Platform
topics: Pull Requests / Merge Requests: description templates, reviewers, labels, milestones
Code review: inline comments, suggestion blocks, approval workflows
Branch protection rules: require PR reviews, status checks, linear history
GitHub Actions overview: workflows, jobs, steps, runners (covered deeply in CI/CD phase)
GitHub Packages: publishing Docker images and packages to GHCR
Issues and project boards: linking PRs to issues, automation
CODEOWNERS file: auto-assign reviewers based on file paths
Secrets management: GitHub Secrets, environment secrets, OIDC for cloud auth
GitLab CI/CD overview: .gitlab-ci.yml, pipelines, runners (if team uses GitLab)

Set up branch protection rules and a CODEOWNERS file on a personal repo

outcomes: Handle any git merge conflict without losing work
Implement and explain trunk-based development to a team

Use git bisect to find the commit that introduced a bug

phases.title: Containerization
phases.subtitle: Docker & Container Fundamentals
phases.description: Containers are the atomic unit of modern DevOps. Docker changed how software is built, shipped, and run. Understanding containers deeply — not just running docker run — is what separates real DevOps engineers from those who just copy commands from Stack Overflow.

sections.title: Docker Core
sections.subtitle: Containers from First Principles
topics: Container vs VM: what containers actually are (namespaces + cgroups), why they are faster
Docker architecture: Docker daemon, Docker CLI, containerd, runc, image layers
Images vs containers: the read-only layer stack, copy-on-write, writable container layer
Docker CLI essentials: run, ps, stop, rm, images, pull, push, exec, logs, inspect
Port mapping: -p hostPort:containerPort, exposing services to the host
Volume mounting: -v and --mount, bind mounts vs named volumes, data persistence
Environment variables: -e, --env-file, injecting config at runtime
Container networking: bridge, host, none — default bridge network, container-to-container DNS
Resource limits: --memory, --cpus — preventing runaway containers
Docker inspect and docker stats: debugging container internals

sections.title: Dockerfile Mastery
sections.subtitle: Building Production-Grade Images
topics: Dockerfile instructions: FROM, RUN, COPY, ADD, WORKDIR, ENV, ARG, EXPOSE, CMD, ENTRYPOINT
CMD vs ENTRYPOINT: the difference, using both together for flexible images
Layer caching: order instructions to maximize cache hits, cache busting
Multi-stage builds: build stage + runtime stage, dramatically smaller images
Base image selection: alpine vs debian-slim vs distroless — size vs compatibility tradeoffs
.dockerignore: excluding node_modules, .git, secrets from build context
Build arguments (ARG) vs runtime environment (ENV): when to use each
Non-root user: running containers as non-root for security (USER instruction)
HEALTHCHECK instruction: defining container health for orchestrators
Image tagging strategy: latest vs semantic versions vs git SHA tags

sections.title: Docker Compose
sections.subtitle: Multi-Container Local Development
topics: docker-compose.yml structure: version, services, networks, volumes
Service definition: image, build, ports, volumes, environment, depends_on
Build context in Compose: building custom images alongside third-party services
Networking in Compose: default network, service discovery by service name
Named volumes vs bind mounts in Compose: persisting database data
Environment files: .env file, variable substitution in compose files
Profiles: conditional service startup for different environments
Health checks and depends_on condition: ensuring startup order correctly
Override files: docker-compose.override.yml for local dev vs CI differences
Compose CLI: up, down, ps, logs, exec, build, pull, restart

sections.title: Container Security
sections.subtitle: Securing Your Containers
topics: Image vulnerability scanning: Trivy, Snyk, Docker Scout — scan before pushing
Minimal base images: attack surface reduction with distroless or alpine
Secrets in containers: never bake secrets into images, use runtime injection
Read-only filesystems: --read-only flag, tmpfs for writable temp dirs
Dropping Linux capabilities: --cap-drop ALL, add back only what's needed
Docker Content Trust (DCT): image signing and verification
Container isolation: understanding what containers share with the host
Rootless Docker: running Docker daemon without root privileges

sections.title: Container Registry
sections.subtitle: Storing and Distributing Images
topics: Docker Hub: public/private repos, rate limits, automated builds
GitHub Container Registry (GHCR): integrating with GitHub Actions workflows
AWS ECR: private registry on AWS, authentication with IAM, lifecycle policies
Google Artifact Registry / GCR: equivalent on GCP
Harbor: self-hosted enterprise registry with security scanning
Image tagging and promotion: dev → staging → prod tagging strategy
Registry authentication: docker login, credential helpers, token rotation

projects: Containerize a full-stack app (frontend + backend + database) with Docker Compose
Write a production-grade multi-stage Dockerfile that produces a minimal image under 50MB

outcomes: Write a production Dockerfile from scratch for any language/framework

phases.title: CI/CD Pipelines
phases.subtitle: Continuous Integration & Continuous Delivery
phases.description: CI/CD is the heart of DevOps. It is the practice of automating the path from a developer's commit to running software in production. Building fast, reliable, and secure pipelines is the core job of a DevOps engineer — this phase makes you dangerous.

sections.title: CI/CD Fundamentals
sections.subtitle: The Philosophy Before the Tools
topics: Continuous Integration: what it means, why small frequent commits beat long-lived branches
Continuous Delivery vs Continuous Deployment: the critical difference
The deployment pipeline: commit → build → test → artifact → deploy → verify
Shift-left testing: catching bugs earlier in the pipeline, not in production
Artifact management: build once, deploy everywhere — immutable artifacts
Feature flags: decoupling deployment from release, dark launches
Deployment strategies: blue/green, canary, rolling update, recreate — when to use each
Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR) vs Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF): the right metrics
Pipeline as Code: why YAML/code-defined pipelines beat GUI-configured ones
The Four Key DORA Metrics: deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, MTTR

sections.title: GitHub Actions
sections.subtitle: The Modern CI/CD Standard
topics: Workflow anatomy: on (triggers), jobs, steps, runs-on, uses, with, env
Triggers: push, pull_request, schedule (cron), workflow_dispatch, repository_dispatch
Runners: GitHub-hosted (ubuntu-latest, windows, macos), self-hosted runners
Actions marketplace: using community actions, pinning to SHA for security
Writing custom actions: JavaScript actions, Docker actions, composite actions
Matrix builds: testing across multiple OS, Node versions, Python versions simultaneously
Caching dependencies: actions/cache for node_modules, pip, Maven, Gradle
Artifacts: actions/upload-artifact, download-artifact — passing files between jobs
Environments and approvals: production environment, required reviewers, deployment gates
OIDC authentication: keyless cloud auth to AWS/GCP/Azure without long-lived secrets
Reusable workflows: calling workflows from other workflows, DRY pipelines
Concurrency groups: preventing overlapping deployments to the same environment

sections.title: Pipeline Patterns
sections.subtitle: Real-World CI/CD Design
topics: Build pipeline: checkout → install deps → lint → test → build → docker build → push
Test stages: unit tests, integration tests, end-to-end tests — parallel execution
Security scanning in CI: SAST (Semgrep, CodeQL), dependency audit (npm audit, Snyk)
Docker image build and push: multi-platform builds (amd64 + arm64) with buildx
GitOps pattern: CI pushes image tag, CD watches repo — separation of concerns
Deployment pipeline: staging deploy → smoke tests → production deploy → health check
Rollback automation: automatic rollback on failed health check post-deploy
Notification integration: Slack/Teams alerts on pipeline failure, deployment success
Pipeline performance: parallelism, caching, incremental builds for fast feedback
Monorepo CI: path-based filtering, only build/test affected services

sections.title: Other CI/CD Tools
sections.subtitle: GitLab CI, Jenkins & ArgoCD
topics: GitLab CI/CD: .gitlab-ci.yml, stages, jobs, artifacts, environments, GitLab runners
Jenkins: Jenkinsfile (declarative vs scripted), plugins ecosystem, agent configuration
Jenkins shared libraries: reusable Groovy code across pipelines
CircleCI: orbs, workflows, resource classes, parallelism
ArgoCD: GitOps continuous delivery for Kubernetes, sync policies, app-of-apps pattern
Flux CD: alternative GitOps tool, Kustomize and Helm integration
Choosing the right tool: GitHub Actions for most teams, Jenkins for enterprise legacy
Pipeline security: secret scanning, SAST, container scanning as pipeline gates

phases.title: Infrastructure as Code
phases.subtitle: Terraform, Ansible & Configuration Management
phases.description: Infrastructure as Code is the practice of managing servers, networks, and cloud resources through code instead of manual clicks. It brings software engineering practices — version control, code review, testing — to infrastructure. This is what makes DevOps engineers 10x more productive than sysadmins.

sections.title: Terraform
sections.subtitle: Infrastructure Provisioning at Scale
topics: Terraform architecture: providers, resources, data sources, state — how it all connects
HCL syntax: blocks, arguments, expressions, functions, type constraints
Core workflow: terraform init → plan → apply → destroy
Providers: AWS, GCP, Azure providers — authentication, version pinning
Resources: declaring cloud resources (EC2, VPC, S3, RDS, etc.)
Data sources: reading existing infrastructure, referencing external state
Variables: input variables (var.), local values (local.), output values
Variable types: string, number, bool, list, map, object, tuple — type constraints
State management: terraform.tfstate, what it is, why it's critical
Remote state: S3 + DynamoDB backend (AWS), GCS backend (GCP) — locking
Terraform workspaces: multiple environments with same code
Modules: creating reusable modules, module sources (registry, git, local)
Module composition: calling modules, passing variables, consuming outputs
Count and for_each: creating multiple resources dynamically
Dynamic blocks: generating repeated nested blocks programmatically
Lifecycle meta-arguments: create_before_destroy, prevent_destroy, ignore_changes
Terraform import: bringing existing infrastructure under Terraform management
Terraform state commands: mv, rm, list, show — managing state directly
Sensitive values: marking outputs as sensitive, handling secrets safely

sections.title: Terraform Best Practices
sections.subtitle: Production-Grade IaC
topics: Directory structure: environments/ vs modules/ — the standard layout
DRY infrastructure: modules for reusable VPC, EKS cluster, RDS patterns
Terragrunt: DRY Terraform configurations across multiple environments
Terraform Cloud / HCP Terraform: remote runs, state, policy as code
CI/CD for Terraform: plan on PR, apply on merge — Atlantis, Terraform Cloud
Drift detection: terraform plan in CI to detect manual changes in cloud
Testing Terraform: Terratest (Go), terraform validate, tflint, checkov for security
Secrets in Terraform: never in state, use Vault provider or SSM Parameter Store
Cost estimation: Infracost — showing cost impact of infrastructure changes in PRs
Terraform upgrade path: tfupgrade, provider version constraints, state migrations

sections.title: Ansible
sections.subtitle: Configuration Management & Application Deployment
topics: Ansible architecture: control node, managed nodes, agentless (SSH-based)
Inventory: static inventory files, dynamic inventory (AWS EC2, GCP), groups
Ad-hoc commands: ansible -m module -a args — quick one-off tasks
Playbooks: YAML structure, plays, tasks, handlers, when conditions
Modules: command, shell, copy, template, file, package, service, user, git
Variables: vars, vars_files, host_vars, group_vars, variable precedence
Templates: Jinja2 templating for config file generation
Roles: directory structure, tasks/main.yml, handlers, templates, defaults, meta
Ansible Galaxy: downloading community roles, requirements.yml
Idempotency: why Ansible tasks must be idempotent and how to ensure it
Vault: encrypting secrets in Ansible (ansible-vault encrypt/decrypt)
Tags: running only specific parts of a playbook
Error handling: ignore_errors, failed_when, rescue blocks
Ansible for server provisioning: bootstrap a fresh server to production-ready state

sections.title: Other IaC Tools
sections.subtitle: Pulumi, CDK & Cloud-Native Options
topics: AWS CloudFormation: stacks, templates (YAML/JSON), changesets, nested stacks
AWS CDK: Infrastructure as actual code (TypeScript/Python), constructs, stacks
Pulumi: multi-cloud IaC in real programming languages (TypeScript, Python, Go)
Crossplane: Kubernetes-native infrastructure provisioning
Chef and Puppet: legacy configuration management — know they exist, why Ansible won
Choosing between tools: Terraform for provisioning, Ansible for configuration — the split

phases.title: Cloud Platforms & Kubernetes
phases.subtitle: AWS/GCP/Azure & Container Orchestration
phases.description: Cloud is where all modern infrastructure lives. Kubernetes is how containers run at scale in production. These two topics are the core of senior DevOps work. Master one cloud deeply, understand the others broadly, and learn Kubernetes well enough to design and operate production clusters.

sections.title: AWS Core Services
sections.subtitle: The World's Dominant Cloud Platform
topics: IAM: users, roles, policies, groups — principle of least privilege, policy simulator
IAM roles for services: EC2 instance profiles, ECS task roles, Lambda execution roles
VPC: subnets (public/private), route tables, internet gateway, NAT gateway, VPC peering
Security Groups vs NACLs: stateful vs stateless, inbound/outbound rules
EC2: instance types, AMIs, key pairs, user data scripts, placement groups
Auto Scaling Groups: launch templates, scaling policies (target tracking, step, scheduled)
Elastic Load Balancing: ALB (HTTP/HTTPS, path/host routing), NLB (TCP), target groups
S3: buckets, objects, versioning, lifecycle rules, cross-region replication, S3 events
S3 security: bucket policies, ACLs, Block Public Access, pre-signed URLs
RDS: managed relational DB, multi-AZ, read replicas, automated backups, parameter groups
ElastiCache: Redis and Memcached managed caching
CloudFront: CDN, origins, behaviors, cache invalidation, signed URLs
Route 53: hosted zones, record types, routing policies (simple, weighted, latency, failover)
Systems Manager (SSM): Parameter Store for secrets, Session Manager (no SSH needed)
CloudWatch: metrics, logs, alarms, dashboards, log insights queries
SNS and SQS: pub/sub messaging, decoupling services, dead letter queues
Lambda: serverless functions, event sources, layers, concurrency, cold starts
ECR: private container registry, image scanning, lifecycle policies
ECS: task definitions, services, Fargate vs EC2 launch type, service discovery

sections.title: AWS Advanced & Other Clouds
sections.subtitle: EKS, Security & Multi-Cloud Awareness
topics: EKS: managed Kubernetes, node groups vs Fargate, addon management, IRSA
AWS Well-Architected Framework: 6 pillars (operational excellence, security, reliability, performance, cost, sustainability)
AWS Organizations: multi-account strategy, SCPs, account vending
Cost management: Cost Explorer, Budgets, Savings Plans, Reserved Instances, Trusted Advisor
GCP equivalents: GKE (EKS), Cloud Run (Lambda+ECS), Cloud SQL (RDS), GCS (S3), Pub/Sub (SNS/SQS)
Azure equivalents: AKS (EKS), Azure Functions (Lambda), Azure SQL (RDS), Blob Storage (S3)
AWS Certifications: Solutions Architect Associate — most valuable DevOps cert, study path

sections.title: Kubernetes Fundamentals
sections.subtitle: Container Orchestration Core Concepts
topics: Kubernetes architecture: control plane (API server, etcd, scheduler, controller manager) vs worker nodes (kubelet, kube-proxy, container runtime)
Pods: the atomic unit, multi-container pods, init containers, sidecar pattern
Deployments: desired state, rollout strategy, rollback, revision history
ReplicaSets: how Deployments manage them, manual scaling
Services: ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer, ExternalName — when to use each
Ingress: Ingress controller (nginx, traefik), host/path routing, TLS termination
ConfigMaps: externalizing configuration from container images
Secrets: base64-encoded secrets, mounting as volumes vs env vars, external secrets
Namespaces: logical isolation, resource quotas, network policies per namespace
PersistentVolumes and PersistentVolumeClaims: storage abstraction, StorageClasses
Resource requests and limits: CPU/memory, VPA vs HPA
Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA): CPU/memory and custom metrics scaling
Node affinity and taints/tolerations: controlling pod placement on nodes
DaemonSets: running a pod on every node (log agents, monitoring agents)
StatefulSets: ordered deployment, stable network identity for databases
Jobs and CronJobs: one-time and scheduled batch workloads

sections.title: Kubernetes Operations
sections.subtitle: Running Clusters in Production
topics: kubectl mastery: get, describe, apply, delete, exec, logs, port-forward, scale, rollout
kubectl contexts: managing multiple clusters, kubeconfig file, kubectx/kubens tools
Helm: package manager for Kubernetes, charts, values files, releases, repositories
Helm chart creation: templates, values.yaml, _helpers.tpl, named templates
Kustomize: overlay-based configuration management, base + overlays pattern
Kubernetes RBAC: Roles, ClusterRoles, RoleBindings, ClusterRoleBindings, service accounts
Network Policies: microsegmentation, restricting pod-to-pod traffic
Pod Disruption Budgets (PDB): ensuring availability during node drains and upgrades
Cluster autoscaler: automatically adding/removing nodes based on pending pods
Vertical Pod Autoscaler (VPA): right-sizing resource requests automatically
Kubernetes upgrades: control plane first, then nodes — zero-downtime upgrade procedure
etcd backup and restore: critical for disaster recovery
Kubernetes debugging: describe, logs, events, exec into pods, ephemeral containers
Service mesh concepts: Istio, Linkerd — mTLS, traffic management, observability
